titlestmt.titleproper: Cornell University College of Engineering photographs16-2-2378
titlestmt.author: Compiled by Laura Linke, December 2007

publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2023-04-05 23:04:47 UTC.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: Cornell University College of Engineering photographs
did.physdesc: Photographs, Maps, Portraits, Negatives, Contact Sheets
did.abstract: College of Engineering photographic negatives, ca. 1945-1965, unidentified; seven lantern slides of field trips, 1870-1900, identified; photographs of Physics Dept. apparatus, 1870s-1880s, identified; five glass negatives and photographs of the construction of the Hydraulic Laboratory; glass slides depicting College of Engineering equipment and facilities (1870-1900); and photographs of John McMullen with a McMullen Scholarship brochure.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Cornell University College of Engineering photographs, #16-2-2378. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: College of Engineering photographic negatives, ca. 1945-1965, unidentified; seven lantern slides of field trips, 1870-1900, identified; photographs of Physics Dept. apparatus, 1870s-1880s, identified; five glass negatives and photographs of the construction of the Hydraulic Laboratory; glass slides depicting College of Engineering equipment and facilities (1870-1900); and photographs of John McMullen with a McMullen Scholarship brochure and electrical engineering photos of equipment, the Arecibo Observatory in Puerto Rico and Professor Victor Colby with his "EE Sculpture" that was installed in Phillips Hall. Includes carte-de-visite photographs of engineering students, 1888, 1893, 1898, 1900, and 1901.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: A number of the photographs from this collection have been digitized and can be seen here: http://rmc-images.library.cornell.edu/ .

did.unittitle: Faculty (multiple names in the folder(s) for each letter: A-P
W. L. Malcolm, Director of Civil Engineering
35th National Convention of Society for the Promotion of Engineering Education, University of Orono, Maine (Rolled Print)
COMEPP (Cornell Manufacturing, Engineering, and Productivity Program)
Earthquakes and National Center for Earthquake Engineering Research
NRRFSS (National Research and Resource Facility for Submicron Structures)
Plasma Studies (LPS: Laboratory of Plasma Studies)
SRC (Semiconductor Research Corporation Program in Microscience and Technology)
Album: Photos of construction of Cornell Hydraulic Laboratory
Alphabetical By Subjects (Folder Titles Supplied By Engineering College)
Students 1970 - Identified (multiple photos: in class; in the lab; on campus; in the dorms)
Students(3 more folders; to be processed)
Prints, Contact Sheets and Negatives (By Photographer or Photo Agency Name)
David Ruether contact sheets and negatives

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: [Note: Engineering College owns copyright to Ruether's images]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Faculty and students group portraits, portrait of unidentified woman,bridges, canals, piers, railroads

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "area covered by the first summer survey of the College of Engineering, Spring 1874" (in Canada; including partial views of the Canadian Pacific RR, the South Saskatchewan River, and several towns; not a field map, but drawn by a member of the class after the survey)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Aerial photos, construction, bridges, buildings, railroads

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Unidentified portrait, College de Genie Civil, Batiment Principal
